mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing equity present values and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening household income and collapsing aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising retail sales and a declining unemployment rate indicate robust demand expansion coincident with labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases over prior 3 months while UNRATE decreases over prior 3 months
failure_condition: In periods where RSAFS rises and UNRATE falls, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a decline in industrial production signals impending earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In late-cycle months with INDPRO declining, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth reflects aggregate demand outperformance that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month nonfarm payroll growth signals expanding household income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS changes are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts signals weakening construction activity and downstream demand for durable goods, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month trailing average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when HOUST is below its 6-month average.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising consumer prices and a near-zero federal funds rate produce deeply negative real interest rates that compress equity discount rates and inflate risk asset valuations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In months with CPIAUCSL rising > 2% annualized and FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations.
